overall_structure.description: The bridge is a rigid, rectangular platform structure designed to span a 10-unit-wide gap between two terrains. It is composed of three sub-structures: a central platform and two extendable arm sub-structures on either side. The central platform serves as the main load-bearing surface, while the two arm sub-structures provide the necessary reach to rest on the two terrains. The arms are connected to the central platform using braces to ensure structural rigidity and stability. The entire structure is built to be slightly longer than 10 units to ensure both ends make contact with the terrain and remain stable under load.
overall_structure.functionality: The bridge functions by providing a continuous, rigid surface across the 10-unit gap. The central platform supports the cargo, while the two arm sub-structures extend to rest on either side of the gap. Braces are used to reinforce the connections between the arms and the central platform for additional strength. Since the cargo is placed gently and its weight increases gradually, the structure must be strong enough to resist bending or collapse under the increasing load.
overall_structure.assembly: The bridge is assembled by first constructing the central platform sub-structure. Then, the two arm sub-structures are built and connected to the left and right ends of the central platform using braces. The arms are extended outward to ensure the entire structure spans the 10-unit gap. The braces ensure that the arms remain aligned and stable during the cargo loading phase.
overall_structure.motion_control: The bridge is initially placed above the gap and allowed to fall gently into position. Once in place, it remains static and does not require any active motion control. The arms and central platform are designed to remain rigid and resist deformation under the increasing cargo weight.

sub_structure_1.name: Central Platform
sub_structure_1.description: A rigid, rectangular platform that serves as the primary load-bearing surface for the cargo. It is built using Small Wooden Blocks arranged in a 2x5 grid to form a 2x5x1 base, with additional layers stacked to increase strength and rigidity.
sub_structure_1.assembly: The blocks are arranged in two layers of 2x5 blocks (total 20 blocks), forming a 2x5x2 platform. This provides a strong, stable base for the cargo to rest on.
sub_structure_1.motion_control: The central platform remains static once the bridge is in place. It does not require any motion control.
sub_structure_1.function: The central platform supports the cargo and distributes its weight evenly across the bridge structure.
sub_structure_1.design_requirements: The platform must be at least 2.5 units wide to accommodate the cargo base. It must be strong enough to resist bending under load. Positions may be micro-adjusted in later stages to resolve conflicts based on actual build execution.

sub_structure_2.name: Left Arm
sub_structure_2.description: An extendable arm constructed from Small Wooden Blocks and reinforced with braces. It is attached to the left side of the central platform and extends approximately 5.5 units to the left to rest on the terrain.
sub_structure_2.components:
Small Wooden Block x 11
        Brace x 5
sub_structure_2.assembly: The arm is constructed using 11 blocks arranged in a 1x11 line. Braces are placed every two blocks to reinforce the structure and prevent sagging under load.
sub_structure_2.motion_control: The left arm remains static once the bridge is in place. It does not require any motion control.
sub_structure_2.function: The left arm provides support by resting on the left terrain and transferring the load from the central platform to the terrain.
sub_structure_2.design_requirements: The arm must extend at least 5 units to ensure it reaches the terrain. It must be rigid and resistant to bending. Positions may be micro-adjusted in later stages to resolve conflicts based on actual build execution.

sub_structure_3.name: Right Arm
sub_structure_3.description: A mirror of the left arm, extending approximately 5.5 units to the right to rest on the right terrain. It is built using the same configuration of blocks and braces as the left arm.
sub_structure_3.components:
Small Wooden Block x 11
        Brace x 5
sub_structure_3.assembly: The arm is constructed using 11 blocks arranged in a 1x11 line. Braces are placed every two blocks to reinforce the structure and prevent sagging under load.
sub_structure_3.motion_control: The right arm remains static once the bridge is in place. It does not require any motion control.
sub_structure_3.function: The right arm provides support by resting on the right terrain and transferring the load from the central platform to the terrain.
sub_structure_3.design_requirements: The arm must extend at least 5 units to ensure it reaches the terrain. It must be rigid and resistant to bending. Positions may be micro-adjusted in later stages to resolve conflicts based on actual build execution.
